title: Luscious American Lamb Roast
description: A juicy, roasted lamb leg with a fragrant herb crust, served with caramelized carrots and onions, perfect for a romantic date night.

ingredients.name: lamb leg roast, bone-in
ingredients.notes: score fat cap in crosshatch pattern

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and let the lamb roast sit at room temperature for 20 minutes.
Score the fat cap of the 1.5‑lb lamb roast in a crosshatch pattern about 1/8‑inch deep, being careful not to cut into the meat.
In a small bowl, combine 2 tbsp olive oil, 4 minced garlic cloves, 2 tbsp fresh rosemary, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper and zest of one lemon; rub this mixture all over the lamb.
Place a rack in a roasting pan and arrange 2 sliced carrots and 1 sliced onion around the lamb.
Roast the lamb for 20 minutes, then lower the oven temperature to 300°F and continue cooking for another 70 minutes, basting with pan juices every 15 minutes.
Check the internal temperature with a meat thermometer; it should reach 145°F for medium‑rare.
Remove the roast from the oven, tent it with foil, and let it rest for 15 minutes before carving.
Meanwhile, transfer the roasted carrots and onions to a skillet and sauté them in a tablespoon of olive oil until tender, about 5 minutes.
Drizzle the pan juices over the vegetables, season with a pinch of sea salt, and serve the sliced lamb on top with the caramelized veggies.
Garnish the plate with a few sprigs of fresh rosemary for an aromatic finish.
